Fix doc-sync workflow: live doc updates, cross-fork PR, reviewer notifications (#4840)

* Fix doc-sync: fallback to opening PR in fork if upstream fails

When the App token can't access the upstream docs repo
(MicrosoftDocs/azure-docs-pr is private), fall back to opening
the PR in the fork instead. The PR can then be manually
submitted upstream.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update docs directly instead of providing a summary

Add update_docs.py script that parses the existing
functions-core-tools-reference.md and patches it in place:
- Adds new option rows to command tables
- Removes option rows for deleted arguments
- Updates descriptions for modified arguments
- Adds new command sections for new commands
- Marks removed commands with deprecation notices
- Updates ms.date in frontmatter

The workflow now commits actual doc changes to the PR branch
instead of dropping summary files into .doc-sync/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix PR creation: use cross-fork URL with manual fallback

The GitHub App token only has access to the fork, not upstream.
Split the monolithic step into:
- Push docs branch (critical, fails the job if broken)
- Create cross-fork PR (best effort, continue-on-error)
- Output PR instructions (always shows clickable URL in
  the workflow step summary for manual PR creation)

The fallback-issue job now only fires on real failures (auth,
push) — not the expected 'can't create PR in upstream' case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add reviewer notification issue with cross-fork PR link

When the automated cross-fork PR creation fails (expected,
since the App token can't access upstream), create a GitHub
Issue in core-tools with:
- A clickable link to create the cross-fork PR manually
- @mentions of reviewers from DOC_SYNC_REVIEWERS variable
- The change summary for context

New repository variable:
- DOC_SYNC_REVIEWERS: comma-separated GitHub usernames
  (defaults to 'liliankasem')

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Document DOC_SYNC_REVIEWERS variable

commit.message:
Fix Emitter Version Dashboard showing unknown commits in shallow clones (#57416)

* Fix Emitter Version Dashboard showing unknown commits in shallow clones

Get-CommitForVersion now falls back to the GitHub API (gh) when local
git log returns no result, which happens in depth-1 sparse checkouts
like the one used by the TypeSpec publish pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use npm publish timestamps for precise commit resolution

Instead of deriving an end-of-day timestamp from the version string
(which can pick up commits made after the CI build), query the npm
registry for the exact publish time of each dependency version.

Also fixes cross-day publishes where the version date differs from
the actual publish date (e.g. 20260320.2 published on 2026-03-21).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle null gh API results and URI-encode path parameter

Normalize the GitHub API result so Hash is null when the API returns
the literal string 'null' (empty commit list). URI-encode the path
parameter to avoid query-string issues with special characters.

commit.message:
Fix memory leak: clean up listeners on repeated useAzureMonitor calls (#1493)

* Fix memory leak: clean up listeners on repeated useAzureMonitor calls

When useAzureMonitor() is called multiple times, old AutoCollectExceptions
and AutoCollectLogs instances were silently overwritten without cleanup,
causing process event listener accumulation and retaining old LogApi
instances (and their downstream spans/connection metadata) in memory.

This adds shutdown of previous instances before re-initialization and
a test verifying listeners do not accumulate.

Fixes: https://github.com/microsoft/ApplicationInsights-node.js/issues/1415

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flaky test infrstructure.

fix: use session-based HTTP transport for multi-client support

The MCP SDK Protocol only allows one transport binding per instance.
The previous stateless approach (sessionIdGenerator: undefined) crashed
when a second connection arrived ('Already connected to a transport').

- initialize creates a new McpServer + transport pair with a UUID session
- subsequent requests reuse the existing pair via Mcp-Session-Id header
- session cleanup on transport close

preserving the exact same public API — all 440 existing tests pass

commit.message:
fix: give generation, verification, and review independent timeouts

Previously, all three eval phases shared a single context timeout. When
generation ran long (e.g. Claude Opus creating 2000+ files), the
verification step would hit 'context deadline exceeded' because the
shared budget was already exhausted.

Each phase now gets its own context.WithTimeout derived from the parent
context:
- GenerateTimeout (default 10m, or --timeout for backward compat)
- VerifyTimeout   (default 5m)
- ReviewTimeout   (default 5m)

New CLI flags: --generate-timeout, --verify-timeout, --review-timeout.
The existing --timeout flag continues to work as the generation timeout.

Removes the minVerifyTimeout workaround in verifier.go since
verification now receives a fresh, independent timeout budget.

Fixes #3

When a new Bluetooth device connects, PulseAudio's module-rescue-streams
can silently move existing audio streams to the newly-appeared sink.
This caused audio to play through the wrong speaker.

Add _correct_other_devices_routing() in bt_monitor.py that fires 3s after
any BT connection event.  It iterates all running subprocesses (except
the one that just connected) and moves misrouted sink-inputs back to their
correct PULSE_SINK via amove_pid_sink_inputs().

Hooked into all connection paths: D-Bus PropertiesChanged signal, D-Bus
reconnect, D-Bus external reconnect, and polling fallback reconnect.

Also adds get_subprocess_pid() to BluetoothManagerHost protocol and
SendspinClient for safe PID access from bt_monitor.

6 new tests covering: skip triggering device, correct misrouted stream,
skip clients without subprocess/sink, handle PulseAudio errors, protocol
method behavior.

commit.message:
docs: add Rh-enamide 4×3 benchmark matrix with preliminary results

Run all 4 backends × 3 optimizers on the 9-molecule Rh-enamide system
(182 parameters) with 2 iterations and 5-minute timeout per combo.

Key findings:
- Nelder-Mead is the only viable optimizer at this scale
- JAX-MD achieves 52.3% improvement in 56s (best early convergence)
- L-BFGS-B diverges on all backends (FD gradients unreliable at 182 params)
- Powell crashes with LinAlgError on JAX engines
- OpenMM/Tinker too slow per eval (all combos timeout at 5 min)
- Tinker has a _write_standalone_prm bug with element '00'

Filed issue #147 to track scaling improvements.

commit.message:
feat: OpenTelemetry MCP Server v1.0.0

Expose your observability stack to AI agents via the Model Context Protocol.

23 tools across 5 domains:
  - Traces (5)  — Jaeger: search, get, services, operations, dependencies
  - Metrics (6) — Prometheus: query, query_range, targets, alerts, metadata, labels
  - Logs (4)    — Loki: query, labels, label_values, trace correlation
  - ZK Proofs (4) — proof get/verify, solvency, stats
  - System (4)  — anomalies, baselines, health, topology

Highlights:
  - Two transports: stdio (Claude Desktop, Copilot) and HTTP (containers)
  - Two-layer auth: backend creds (Bearer/Basic per backend) + client API keys
  - Container-native: env-var config, K8s Secret mounting, multi-stage Dockerfile
  - Selective tool groups: --tools traces,metrics,logs
  - Zero vulnerabilities, zero runtime deps beyond MCP SDK + Zod
